steps.title: Locate the strongest clue
steps.content: The phrase “This incident leads me to the reflection” explicitly shows how the underlined sentence is being used: as a specific example that introduces and supports a general observation about imagination and reality.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
The underlined sentence provides a specific event—writing about an imaginary voyage in 1845, then visiting NYC in reality—that exemplifies the phenomenon the next sentence identifies: imagination foreshadowing reality.

The other choices either misread his emotional development (A), invent publishing concerns (B), or mislabel what the example illustrates (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
